fact: April 28 is exactly 118 days into the year - that's almost one-third of the whole year gone!
In the Northern Hemisphere, dandelions are typically exploding everywhere right now, turning into those fluffy white puffballs you can blow wishes on.
The sun is usually rising about 2 minutes earlier each day around this time in northern regions, making mornings feel much brighter!
Baby rabbits born in early April are often hopping around like tiny fuzzy ping-pong balls by now - they're about 3 weeks old and super bouncy.
This date falls in the middle of Taurus season, which means the zodiac bull is charging through the sky above us.
Tree leaves in temperate regions are typically that perfect bright green color right now - not the dark summer green yet, but that glowing lime green that almost hurts your eyes.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this is often prime spider season as huntsman spiders get extra active when winter approaches.
When there's a new moon around April 28, the night sky is perfect for seeing shooting stars since there's no moonlight to block them.
Earthworms are usually super active right now! The soil temperature is just right for them to wiggle around like crazy after spring rains.
In northern places like Canada, ice on lakes often makes weird groaning and cracking sounds as it finally melts - like the lake is waking up from a long nap.
April 28 is exactly 4 months away from both Christmas (December 28) and late August - it's like a perfect middle point in the year.
